source_info.section: Development of a combined model on the basis of the SOFA score

population: Pediatric patients with sepsis (Klebsiella pneumoniae-related)
method: ROC analysis, DeLong's test, integrated discrimination improvement index
performance: AUC 0.89 (95% CI 0.82–0.96), sensitivity 0.86, specificity 0.84, PPV 0.64, NPV 0.95; outperformed original SOFA (DeLong's test P<0.01)
notes: Selected as optimal multivariate predictive model; used to develop Kp-specific SOFA scoring system with prior hospitalization assigned 4 points

source_info.section: Development of a combined model on the basis of the SOFA score

population: Patients with Klebsiella pneumoniae infection
method: Kaplan-Meier survival curves with log-rank test and hazard ratio analysis
effect_size: Medium-risk vs low-risk: HR = 8.36 (95% CI 3.60–27.83); High-risk vs low-risk: HR = 20.27 (95% CI 7.47–54.95)
notes: Mean estimated risk score for study population was 8 ± 4 (range 0–21). Score components include intra-abdominal source (4 points). Score stratifies patients into three risk groups.

source_info.section: Development of a combined model on the basis of the SOFA score

population: Pediatric patients
notes: 30-day mortality rate was 17.1% (25/146)

population: Pediatric patients
notes: Three risk categories by Kaplan-Meier survival curves with predetermined cut-off values showed significant difference in 30-day mortality among three strata by log-rank test (P < 0.01)

source_info.section: Discussion

population: Pediatric patients with Klebsiella pneumoniae bloodstream infection (Kp-BSI)
performance: AUC not explicitly stated for original SOFA alone; difference in AUC vs Kp-specific SOFA significant (P < 0.01); specificity 0.68
notes: Compared against Kp-specific SOFA score using DeLong's test

source_info.section: Discussion

population: Pediatric patients with Klebsiella pneumoniae bloodstream infection (Kp-BSI)
method: Multivariate logistic regression and ROC analysis
effect_size: SOFA score coefficient: 0.53; prior hospitalization coefficient: 1.81; intra-abdominal source coefficient: 1.83
notes: Kp-specific SOFA score significantly outperformed original SOFA score (DeLong's test P < 0.01); score also stratified patients into three mortality strata

source_info.section: Discussion

population: Pediatric patients with Klebsiella pneumoniae bloodstream infection (Kp-BSI)
notes: None

source_info.section: Discussion

population: Pediatric patients with Klebsiella pneumoniae bloodstream infection (Kp-BSI)
sample_size: N=146 (93 low risk, 28 medium risk, 25 high risk)
predictor: Risk score category (Low risk ≤8, Medium risk 9–11, High risk ≥12)
effect_size: Medium risk HR 8.36 (95% CI 3.60–27.83); High risk HR 20.27 (95% CI 7.47–54.95); Low risk = reference
performance: Medium risk p<0.01; High risk p<0.01
notes: Estimated risk of mortality: Low risk ≤16.24%, Medium risk 24.73–48.55%, High risk ≥61.53%. Observed mortality: Low risk 5/93 (5.4%), Medium risk 10/28 (35.7%), High risk 18/25 (72.0%). Overall 90-day mortality in study was 22.6%.

population: Children with Klebsiella pneumoniae bloodstream infection (Kp-BSI)
notes: Higher SOFA scores associated with increased risk of mortality; three risk categories defined by score ≤8, 9–11, and ≥12

source_info.section: Discussion

population: Children with Klebsiella pneumoniae bloodstream infection (Kp-BSI), mostly healthcare-associated infection with various comorbidities
method: ROC analysis (AUC), integrated discrimination improvement analysis
performance: AUC higher than standard SOFA (0.80); negative predictive value 0.95
notes: Kp-specific SOFA developed by adding two risk factors as sub-scores to standard SOFA; adding CRP and albumin provided only minimal additional advantage over Kp-specific SOFA per IDI analysis

source_info.section: Conclusion

population: Patients with bloodstream infection caused by carbapenem-resistant Klebsiella pneumoniae (CR-Kp)
method: ROC analysis / Cox proportional hazards model
performance: Best predictive performance among single predictors (specific metrics not reported in excerpt)
notes: Combination of SOFA with prior hospitalization, intra-abdominal source of infection, C-reactive protein levels, and albumin improved predictive performance. Final model: SOFA + prior hospitalization + intra-abdominal source of infection.

method: Kaplan-Meier analysis (log-rank test) and ROC analysis
notes: Kp-specific SOFA score compared to original SOFA score for predicting 30-day mortality (Figure S1). Survival probabilities estimated by Kaplan-Meier analysis (Figure S2).
